parts.answer: Define ÔÇÿcompetitive pricingÔÇÖ. Award 2 marks for a full definition. Award 1 mark for a partial definition. When a product is priced in line or just below competitorsÔÇÖ prices (to try to capture more of the market) [2] OR Setting a price similar to that of competitors products which are already established in the market. [2] OR When the price of a good is set at the same level as other products in the market and at a level customers expect or are willing to pay [2] Partial definition e.g. have similar prices to other businesses [1] OR bases their prices on the competitors pricing [1]

Define ÔÇÿmarketing budgetÔÇÖ. Award 2 marks for a full definition. Award 1 mark for a partial definition. A financial plan for the marketing of a product or product range (for a specified period of time) [2] OR The amount of money made available by a business for its marketing activities during a particular period of time [2] Partial definition e.g. money to be spent on marketing [1] 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Outline two possible threats to PamelaÔÇÖs business of using e-commerce. Award 1 mark for each relevant threat (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant reference to this business (max 2). Points might include: ´éÀ Increased competition [k] which makes it harder to calculate a competitive price [app] ´éÀ Unfamiliarity / customers are less likely to buy from a business they donÔÇÖt know [k] leading to fewer sales of toys [app] ´éÀ No face-to-face contact which could make it difficult to establish a relationship [k] so difficult to gain customer loyalty [app] ´éÀ Website design must be clear / attractive / easy to use, or customers will not visit [k] ´éÀ Limited/no access to the internet [k] ´éÀ Increasing costs as a specialist may need to be employed / website design / maintenance of sites [k] ´éÀ May need a larger space to store products increasing costs [k] ´éÀ Packaging/delivery of each item will increase costs per product [k] ´éÀ Larger number of returns could lead to higher business costs [k] ´éÀ All customers can see negative reviews [k] Other appropriate responses can also be credited. To use words from the stem as application, the reference must be appropriate (i.e. make sense) in relation to the point being made. The following words are likely to be appropriate for this question: ´éÀ Gift shop ´éÀ (celebration) Cards/toys/candy ´éÀ Entrepreneur/the owner ´éÀ 30 years ago ´éÀ 5 employees ´éÀ Customer loyalty ´éÀ Competitive pricing ´éÀ Marketing budget ´éÀ Sales promotion Other appropriate examples in context can still be credited. Can outline two separate costs if both clearly related to Pamela/business. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Explain two methods PamelaÔÇÖs business can use to maintain customer loyalty. Award 1 mark for identification of each relevant method (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant reference to this business (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant explanation (max 2). Points might include: ´éÀ Communicate with customers e.g. newsletters, social media [k] so that they remember your business [an] when they next need candy (sweets) / toys / cards [app] ´éÀ Offer rewards to existing customers / introduce a loyalty scheme [k] using money from the marketing budget [app] so consumers have an incentive to return [an] ´éÀ Train staff / offer good quality service [k] from all 5 employees [app] ´éÀ Extra services or examples such as delivery / credit facilities [k] ´éÀ Ask for feedback / carry out market research [k] so the items you are selling match customer needs [an] ´éÀ Resolve complaints quickly [k] so that customers donÔÇÖt go to other businesses [an] ´éÀ Introduce new products / high quality products [k] satisfying the customersÔÇÖ needs [an] ´éÀ Brand loyalty [k] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. To use words from the stem as application, the reference must be appropriate (i.e. make sense) in relation to the point being made. The following words are likely to be appropriate for this question: ´éÀ Gift shop ´éÀ (celebration) Cards/toys/candy ´éÀ 5 employees ´éÀ Entrepreneur ´éÀ 30 years ago ´éÀ Competitive pricing ´éÀ Marketing budget ´éÀ Sales promotion Other appropriate examples in context can still be credited. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Do you think being a risk-taker is a more important characteristic of a successful entrepreneur than being creative? Justify your answer. Award up to 2 marks for identification of relevant points. Award up to 2 marks for relevant development of points. Award 2 marks for a justified decision as to whether being a risk-taker is a more important characteristic of a successful entrepreneur than being creative. Points might include: Risk-taker: ´éÀ A successful entrepreneur must take risks otherwise there is no business [k] ´éÀ Entrepreneurs take the risk of investing their own money [k] this may bring high rewards [an] however they may fail and lose their personal possessions [an] ´éÀ Taking risks is a way to learn [k] if an idea is good or not [an] Creative: ´éÀ New ideas [k] leading to increased sales/revenue [an] ´éÀ Creativity adds value [k] so higher prices can be charged [an]. ´éÀ Creativity alone does not guarantee success [k] if customers do not purchase [an] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. Justification might include: Entrepreneurs take the risk of investing their own money [k] this may bring high rewards [an]. Being creative maybe a more important characteristic as making a business different to its competitors [k] can lead to increased sales [an]. In a competitive market creativity is especially important because it helps the business stand out from competitors. [eval] An entrepreneur can take risks, but if customers will not buy, they will not be successful. [eval] This is a general question so there are no marks for application. For evaluation to be awarded justification will usually follow on from relevant analysis of points. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Define ÔÇÿpublic limited companyÔÇÖ. Award 2 marks for a full definition. Award 1 mark for a partial definition. Businesses owned by shareholders, but they can sell shares to the public and their shares are tradeable on the stock exchange [2] OR Often a large company, owned by shareholders who have limited liability. The company can sell its shares to the general public [2] OR A company that sells its shares to the general public through a stock exchange (because the company can sell shares to anyone, it can raise massive amounts of money) [2] OR A business whose shares can be freely bought and sold to the general public [2] Partial definition e.g. a business that can sell shares [1]

Calculate the margin of safety for CTFÔÇÖs childrenÔÇÖs beds. Show your working. Correct answer: 4000 [2] Correct method but incorrect answer e.g. Current output ´Ç¡ break-even output [1] OR 18 000 ÔÇô 14 000 [1] = 4000 [1] If correct answer given with no working shown, award 2 marks. Award only 1 mark if 4000 is written as part of the answer but is not given as the final answer. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Outline two ways an increase in inflation might affect CTF. Award 1 mark for each relevant way (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant reference to this business (max 2). Points might include: ´éÀ Employees might ask for pay rise [k] reducing its margin of safety [app] ´éÀ Increase in raw material costs[k] for manufacturing (beds) [app] ´éÀ Prices may have to increase [k] ´éÀ Fewer sales / customers cannot afford to buy [k] the beds [app] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. To use words from the stem as application, the reference must be appropriate (i.e. make sense) in relation to the point being made. The following words are likely to be appropriate for this question: ´éÀ Public limited company (PLC) ´éÀ (childrenÔÇÖs) Beds ´éÀ Factory/manufacturer ´éÀ Margin of safety / 4000 ´éÀ Batch production ´éÀ Environmental pressures ´éÀ 000 (break-even output) ´éÀ 000 (current output) ´éÀ 000 (capacity) Other appropriate examples in context can still be credited. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Explain two possible advantages to CTF of using batch production. Award 1 mark for identification of each relevant advantage (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant reference to this business (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant explanation (max 2) Points might include: ´éÀ Flexible [k] easy to change the type of beds [app] to adapt to customer demands [an] ´éÀ Economies of scale / materials can be bought in bulk [k] which can help reduce average costs [an] improving the margin of safety [app] ´éÀ Production may not be affected to any great extent if machinery breaks down [k] so some output made [an] so help reach its break-even output of 14 000 [app] ´éÀ Variety of products [k] which could lead to increased sales [an] ´éÀ Variety of tasks can motivate workers [k] leading to less absenteeism / lower labour turnover [an] at the factory [app] ´éÀ Spread risk (as can make more than one type of product) [k] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. To use words from the stem as application, the reference must be appropriate (i.e., make sense) in relation to the point being made. The following words are likely to be appropriate for this question: ´éÀ Public limited company (PLC) ´éÀ (childrenÔÇÖs) Beds ´éÀ Factory/manufacturer ´éÀ Margin of safety / 4000 ´éÀ Environmental pressures ´éÀ 000 (break-even output) ´éÀ 000 (current output) ´éÀ 000 (capacity) Other appropriate examples in context can still be credited. Advantages should focus on advantages to the business not employees. Answers such as variety of work on own are too vague [tv]. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Do you think manufacturing businesses should always respond to environmental pressures? Justify your answer. Award up to 2 marks for identification of relevant points. Award up to 2 marks for relevant development of points. Award 2 marks for a justified decision as to whether manufacturing businesses should always respond to environmental pressures. Points might include: ´éÀ Reduces the risk of a customer boycott [k] ´éÀ Enhance brand image / reputation [k] leading to more potential customers / revenue [an] ´éÀ Help recruit / retain employees [k] ´éÀ Help attract investors [k] so raise more capital [an] ´éÀ Reduces risk of legal action/fines [k] avoiding bad publicity[an] ´éÀ May need to buy new equipment [k] which increases costs [an] leading to higher prices [an] ´éÀ Reduce productivity [k] which could lower profit margin [an] ´éÀ May need to find new suppliers of raw materials [k] leading to higher unit costs [an] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. Justification might include: Yes, although this could mean the business has to find new suppliers of raw materials [k] increasing unit costs [an]. However, responding to environmental pressures can improve the reputation of the business [k] leading to an increase in revenue [an]. The additional revenue gained could offset the costs of responding to environmental pressures. So, if they can find the resources they need at a competitive price, they should. [eval] [eval] This is a general question so there are no marks for application. For evaluation to be awarded justification will usually follow on from relevant analysis of points. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Define ÔÇÿshareholdersÔÇÖ equityÔÇÖ . Award 2 marks for a full definition. Award 1 mark for a partial definition. (Total sum of) money invested into the business by the owners of the company [2] OR Share capital plus reserves [2] OR Total assets minus total liabilities [2] Partial definition e.g. money raised from selling shares [1]

Identify two financial statements a public limited company is legally required to publish each year. Award 1 mark for each financial statement. Two from: ´éÀ Income statement (Profit and loss) ´éÀ Statement of financial position (Balance sheet) ´éÀ Cash-flow statement/statement of cash flows ´éÀ Statement of changes in equity Only award the first two responses given. Do not award cash-flow forecast.

State whether each of the following are classified as a current asset or a current liability. Award 1 mark for each correct answer. Overdraft ´Ç¡ current liability Trade receivables ´Ç¡ current asset Trade payables ´Ç¡ current liability Cash in bank ´Ç¡ current asset 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Explain one way each of the following stakeholder groups might use VCGÔÇÖs accounts: Award 1 mark for identification of each relevant way (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant reference to this business (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant explanation (max 2). Points might include: Shareholders: ´éÀ Help assess business performance / profits [k] to see whether they will receive a dividend / return on their investment [an] from this bag manufacturer [app] ´éÀ Help decide whether to continue to invest [k] as the business is looking to open a second factory [app] which could mean less dividends paid [an] ´éÀ Assess the liquidity [k] they do not want to invest in a business with serious cash or liquidity problems [an] ´éÀ To compare performance over time [k] and how much the $800 000 in equity increases [app] to decide whether they should take their money and invest elsewhere [an] ´éÀ Compare performance between different businesses [k] to decide whether it is a good investment [an] Suppliers: ´éÀ Can see the total value / level of debt [k] current liabilities $250 000 [app] to help decide whether they should offer (more) credit [an] ´éÀ Assess whether the business has sufficient cash [k] within current assets of $300 000 [app] for the business to be able to repay them when debts become due [an] ´éÀ Assess profitability / level of sales [k] successful business may need more materials so maintains or increase revenue for the supplier [an] especially as make high-quality products [app] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. To use words from the stem as application, the reference must be appropriate (i.e., make sense) in relation to the point being made. The following words are likely to be appropriate for this question: ´éÀ Bags or examples such as briefcase / travel bag ´éÀ (second) Factory ´éÀ $2m (revenue) ´éÀ $800 000 (equity) ´éÀ $300 000 (current assets) ´éÀ $250 000 (current liabilities) ´éÀ 1.2 (current ratio) ´éÀ High ´Ç¡ quality ´éÀ 50 / Skilled workers Other appropriate examples

Other appropriate responses should also be credited. Justification might include: Skilled labour may be difficult to find [k] which could increase recruitment costs [an] Factory machinery can replace some labour [k] reducing the importance of labour in the location decision [an]. Labour is the most important factor if the business is labour intensive because without labour the new factory cannot produce output [eval] however, other factors would be of greater importance in a new capital-intensive factory. [eval] 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Identify two objectives for a business organisation in the public sector. Award 1 mark per objective. Points might include: ´éÀ Financial or examples e.g. meet targets set by government, improve efficiency/reduce costs ´éÀ Make product/service affordable to all ´éÀ Provide a service to everyone ´éÀ Protect or create jobs in certain areas (social objectives) Other appropriate responses should also be credited. Only award the first two responses given.

Define ÔÇÿchain of commandÔÇÖ. Award 2 marks for a full definition. Award 1 mark for a partial definition. The structure in an organisation which allows instructions to be passed down from senior management to lower levels of management [2] OR The route through which authority is passed down through an organisation [2] OR Shows how communication and tasks are passed down the business from the top to the bottom [2] Partial definition e.g. shows who gives orders [1] 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Outline two ways MBH can use ideas from HerzbergÔÇÖs theory to motivate its employees. Award 1 mark for each relevant point. Award 1 mark for each relevant reference to this business. Points might include: ´éÀ Could give employees more responsibility [k] in the hospital [app] ´éÀ Offer more interesting work [k] as many are skilled employees [app] ´éÀ Offer more praise for good work [k] ´éÀ Give chances for promotion [k] as there is a tall organisational structure [app] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. To use words from the stem as application, the reference must be appropriate (i.e. make sense) in relation to the point being made. The following words are likely to be appropriate for this question: ´éÀ 440 / doctors ´éÀ (5) Hospitals / or related words ´éÀ 5600 employees ´éÀ Public sector ´éÀ Communication barriers ´éÀ Phone/email ´éÀ Long chain of command ´éÀ Tall organisational structure ´éÀ Skilled employees Other appropriate examples in context can still be credited. Do not award examples of hygiene factors on their own e.g., Pay, improve working conditions as these are not motivators 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Explain how MBH might reduce or remove each of the following communication barriers : Award 1 mark for identification of each relevant way (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant reference to this business (max 2). Award 1 mark for each relevant explanation (max 2). Points might include: Messages lost: ´éÀ Ask for feedback [k] to ensure that the message is received [an] by all 5 hospitals [app] ´éÀ Make sure the medium of communication is suitable/works [k] ´éÀ Delayering/ flattening the structure [k] because it is a long chain of command [app] Use of jargon: ´éÀ Use simple language [k] on the phone/email [app] so easy for everyone / receiver to understand [an] ´éÀ Make sure language is appropriate to the receiver [k] to make sure there is no misunderstanding [an] ´éÀ Providing opportunities for the receiver to ask questions [k] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. To use words from the stem as application, the reference must be appropriate (i.e. make sense) in relation to the point being made. The following words are likely to be appropriate for this question: ´éÀ 440 / doctors ´éÀ (5) Hospitals / or related words ´éÀ 5600 employees ´éÀ Public sector ´éÀ Phone/email ´éÀ Long chain of command ´éÀ Tall organisational structure ´éÀ Externally recruited Other appropriate examples in context can still be credited. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes

Do you think an organisation should always use external recruitment when recruiting skilled employees? Justify your answer. Award up to 2 marks for identification of relevant points Award up to 2 marks for relevant development of points. Award 2 marks for a justified decision as to whether an organisation should always use external recruitment when recruiting skilled employees. Points might include: External recruitment: ´éÀ Bring in new skills / ideas [k] which could help improve productivity / effectiveness / efficiency of business [an] ´éÀ Can bring in valuable knowledge and experience of competition [k] gaining a competitive edge [an] ´éÀ Wider choice of applicants [k] increases possibility of finding best person for job [an] ´éÀ No knowledge of how business works / current operations [k] so might need induction training [an] ´éÀ Avoids risk of upsetting employees when someone internal is promoted [k] ´éÀ Higher recruitment costs [k] ´éÀ More time spent / takes longer to fill vacancy [k] ´éÀ Required if no suitable candidates in the business [k] Internal recruitment : ´éÀ Can help motivate other employees [k] which could lead to fewer people leaving / reduce absenteeism [an] ´éÀ Internal promotion can create another vacancy that then needs to be filled [k] Other appropriate responses should also be credited. This is a general question so there are no marks for application. For evaluation to be awarded justification will usually follow on from relevant analysis of points. Advantages of external recruitment can be disadvantages of internal but do not award the same point twice as mirror answers are not accepted. 0450/11 May/June 2022 Notes External recruitment can bring in new skills and ideas [k] helping improve productivity [an]. Whereas using internal recruitment can help motivate other employees [k] which could lead to fewer people leaving [an]. Overall, it depends on the skills within the business because if there are no suitable workers external recruitment would be the only ch
